mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports corporate earnings and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 3.7% in June 2019 shows tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with positive PAYEMS growth are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.4% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when this threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 2.38% in June 2019, near restrictive territory
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a coordinated demand-supply contraction that triggers risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.56 and RSAFS at 519020 in May 2019, both showing signs of plateauing
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where unemployment is below 4% and interest rates are elevated, a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because consumer spending exhaustion is interpreted as a recession precursor.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and FEDFUNDS at 2.38% in mid-2019 indicate late-cycle conditions
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE < 4.0% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, declines in RSAFS do not produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than in other regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces a rapid repricing of near-term Fed policy expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as real discount rates adjust.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2019 CPI at 255.3 showing persistent inflation pressure
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL changes and 5-day forward returns is above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rising CPI are not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in new housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand strength in interest-rate-sensitive sectors and upstream construction activity, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1269 thousand units in May 2019 showing steady residential construction
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate immediate supply chain disruptions and inventory drawdowns that trigger rapid de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.56 in May 2019 showing industrial sector volatility
failure_condition: Instances of INDPRO declining > 0.5% month-over-month do not produce negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint elevation of nominal interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) determines a high real policy rate that squeezes corporate margins and depresses equity valuations over quarterly horizons, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2019 CPI at 255.3 and Fed funds at 2.38% imply positive real rates
failure_condition: Joint elevation of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
